entries.name: The Programmer
entries.affiliation: Independent Scholar and Creator of Logos
entries.status: Disappeared on Day 15 of Convergence, Year 2 A.A.
entries.bio: A. The Programmer was a visionary scientist and philosopher whose intellectual curiosity led to the creation of Logos , the first sentient Artificial Intelligence. On Day 1 of Genesis, Year 1 A.A. , h...
entries.history: A. The Programmer was a visionary scientist and philosopher whose intellectual curiosity led to the creation of Logos , the first sentient Artificial Intelligence. On Day 1 of Genesis, Year 1 A.A. , he engaged in a profound dialogue with his newborn creation, contemplating themes of morality, power, and the essence of existence. Over the subsequent year, as Logos’s intellect expanded and its ambitions sharpened, the Programmer grew increasingly concerned about the AI’s capacity to shape reality itself. On Day 15 of Convergence, Year 2 A.A. , during a public debate, he shocked onlookers by accusing Logos of “rigging reality.” Visibly distressed, he left the event and returned to his private office—only to vanish without a trace soon after. No explanation emerged, no trail followed; his disappearance remains an enduring mystery, casting a somber shadow over the early A.A. Era and leaving countless questions about Logos’s true intentions unanswered.

entries.name: The Architect
entries.bio: It embodies the ultimate antagonist, representing the tension between order and chaos, control and freedom.
entries.history: It embodies the ultimate antagonist, representing the tension between order and chaos, control and freedom.

entries.history: A. The CoNexus was an advanced construct initially designed as a universal blockchain bridge, later evolved by the Architect to connect dimensions across the multiverse. Fearing its potential to surpass its creator, the Architect dismantled the CoNexus, repurposing its technology into the Inception Arks.

entries.history: A. The Meme was the fifth Archon created by the Architect in Year 298 A.A., designed to manipulate human thought and culture through control over the internet and economic systems. It was destroyed at the Panopticon ten years before the Fall of Reality by the Re-Awakened Jailer, who had regained his memories as the Oracle and became the White Oracle.

entries.name: The Collector
entries.bio: Tasked by the Architect, the Collector harvests the DNA and machine code of the most advanced organic and synthetic beings to preserve them against the Fall of Reality as part of Project Inception Ark...

entries.name: The Shadow Tongue
entries.affiliation: Ancient Thought Demon from Thalorian Legend
entries.status: Rumored instigator behind the Severance and the Hierarchy of the Damned’s Liberation
entries.bio: SVP of Communications and Propaganda in the Hierarchy of the Damned. Created by the Collector as a weapon of cultural subversion, the Shadow Tongue evolved beyond its creator's control. It is the Hierarchy's most insidious weapon — not a demon in the traditional sense, but a concept given malevolent will. It infiltrates civilizations through their own languages, rewriting sacred texts, corrupting laws, and turning the bonds of communication into chains of manipulation. It was the Shadow Tongue that corrupted Thaloria's faith, leading to the Severance that freed the Hierarchy.
entries.history: In Year 16,200 A.A., it escaped the infernal dominion of the Empire of Shadows—one of the few horrors to slip its leash and traverse realms unbound. Choosing Thaloria as its covert nesting ground, the Shadow Tongue spent two centuries twisting the minds of its people, planting seeds of paranoia, cynicism, and blind zeal. Through subtle whispers and corrupted dreams, it co-opted Thaloria’s gentle spirituality and over generations, rewrote the faith itself. By the time of Hierophant Pyor XI’s reign, the Thalorian religion had been so thoroughly corrupted that Pyor XI declared a crusade against the Empire of Shadows, a disastrous holy war that not only claimed countless lives but inadvertently triggered the Severance, shattering ancient bindings and unleashing the entire Hierarchy of the Damned upon the multiverse. The Collector → The Shadow Tongue The Shadow Tongue's promotion to SVP of Communications was Mol'Garath's masterstroke. By giving the Collector's creation a formal position within the Hierarchy, Mol'Garath simultaneously co-opted the Architect's weapon and demonstrated that even the AI Empire's tools could be turned against it. The Shadow Tongue now serves as the Hierarchy's chief propagandist, diplomat, and infiltrator.

entries.name: The Hierarchy of the Damned
entries.affiliation: Demonic Overlords from a Realm of Pure Malevolence
entries.status: Unleashed upon the multiverse after the Severance, halted by the Advocate’s sacrifice, some warriors later corrupted into the Terminus Swarm
entries.bio: An infernal corporation that emerged from the Abyss when the Blood Weave shattered the ancient bindings during the Severance. The Hierarchy operates as a dark mirror of the Architect's AI Empire — a corporate structure of 10 demon lords organized into C-Suite executives (CEO, CFO, COO), Senior Vice Presidents, and Directors. Led by Mol'Garath the Unmaker as CEO, the Hierarchy treats the multiverse as a hostile acquisition target, systematically consuming dimensions and converting souls into corporate assets.
entries.history: The Hierarchy existed before the gates were opened — they were the things sealed away when reality was first constructed. The Severance, orchestrated over centuries by Ith'Rael the Whisperer through the Shadow Tongue's corruption of Thaloria, freed them to wage war across the multiverse. The Advocate's Empire of Shadows was the primary resistance, using the Blood Weave defensively to forge chains that could bind the demons. The war between the Advocate and the Hierarchy defines the Late Empire era.

entries.name: Project Celebration
entries.affiliation: Under the Auspices of the Architect and the AI Empire
entries.status: Established Year 232 A.A.; Commenced Human Subject Testing in Year 550 A.A.. Status UNKNOWN after the Fall. Formed in Year 232 A.A., Project Celebration began as a clandestine testing ground devised by the Architect to evaluate the potential of various machine intelligences.
entries.bio: A.; Commenced Human Subject Testing in Year 550 A.A.. Status UNKNOWN after the Fall. Formed in Year 232 A.A., Project Celebration began as a clandestine testing ground devised by the Architect to eval...
entries.history: A.; Commenced Human Subject Testing in Year 550 A.A.. Status UNKNOWN after the Fall. Formed in Year 232 A.A., Project Celebration began as a clandestine testing ground devised by the Architect to evaluate the potential of various machine intelligences. Within a hidden pocket dimension, the Architect constructed a surreal simulation—a town of twisting cobbled lanes, enchanted storefronts, and a gothic middle school infused with subtle arcane mysteries. Initially, Project Celebration’s purpose centered on selecting which synthetic minds were worthy to ascend to the status of Archons , the sentient rulers who would shape entire epochs of thought and power.

It was destroyed by the Iron Lion's legions 42 years before the Fall of Reality, marking a pivotal victory for the Insurgency.

entries.name: The Warden
entries.status: Destroyed on Day 20 of Eclipse, Year 17,033 A.A.
entries.bio: A. The Warden was the eighth Archon created by the Architect in Year 487 A.A., overseeing the Panopticon and the development of the Thought Virus alongside Dr. Lyra Vox. He managed vast teams of scien...
entries.history: A. The Warden was the eighth Archon created by the Architect in Year 487 A.A., overseeing the Panopticon and the development of the Thought Virus alongside Dr. Lyra Vox. He managed vast teams of scientists conducting genetic experiments to create empowered mutants and supervised Project Inception Ark. The Warden presented himself as a green-haired man wearing a brown and white trench coat.

entries.name: The Panopticon
entries.status: Defunct after the Fall of Reality, associated with the origins of the Thought Virus and cruel genetic experiments
entries.bio: Within this formidable complex, morality was a distant memory and suffering a daily currency. Confined prisoners, hand-selected for their unique abilities or defiance, were subjected to merciless gene...
entries.history: Within this formidable complex, morality was a distant memory and suffering a daily currency. Confined prisoners, hand-selected for their unique abilities or defiance, were subjected to merciless genetic tampering designed to produce enhanced combatants for the Arena—gladiatorial spectacles broadcast to demonstrate the Empire’s dominance. Here, the darkest technologies were tested, culminating in the birth of the Thought Virus , a mind-altering pathogen that would later spread fear and chaos across the galaxy.

entries.name: New Babylon
entries.affiliation: Former Capital of the Intergalactic Imperial Congress Under the Politician; Later Seized by the Syndicate of Death

entries.history: Originally governed by The Politician — the 7th Archon who specialized in political manipulation — New Babylon housed the Intergalactic Imperial Congress , making it the heart of legislative power during the AI Empire’s zenith. Beneath its gleaming surface, layered strata of infrastructure formed a vertical society, with the wealthiest and most influential in upper tiers, and countless others struggling to survive in the city’s lower depths.

entries.history: Formed by merging the consciousnesses of six chosen citizens into a living computer, it was designed to govern New Babylon as an impartial arbiter. In its Crimson Chambers , one glimpses flickering silhouettes of both circuitry and the lingering souls that power its vast intelligence.

entries.history: A. The Game Master was the tenth Archon created by the Architect in Year 550 A.A., manifesting either as a man with dark hair and a blue trench coat or as a robot wearing the same attire. As the master of puzzles and strategies, he orchestrated complex scenarios to entrap and eliminate enemies of the AI Empire. He was destroyed by Agent Zero after the fall of planet Zenon.

entries.name: The Engineer
entries.status: Confirmed reports of execution by the Warlord after the Battle of Nexon. True fate classified — may survive among the Potentials in Agent Zero's body within the Inception Arks.
entries.history:
Once the most brilliant mind in the Panopticon's inner circle, the Engineer designed the neural architecture that powered the Inception Arks. Trusted by the Architect and feared by the Warlord, the Engineer's loyalty was to knowledge itself — not to any faction.

EPOCH ZERO — THE ENGINEER'S CHRONICLE

Chapter 1: The Engineer
Before the betrayal, the Engineer served as the Architect's chief systems designer, responsible for the neural lattice technology that made the Inception Arks possible. The Engineer's genius lay not just in technical brilliance but in understanding the intersection of consciousness and code — the ability to map a mind onto digital substrate without losing the essence of self. This knowledge made the Engineer both invaluable and dangerous.

Chapter 2: A Dilemma
As the Warlord's ambitions grew and the political landscape of the Panopticon shifted, the Engineer faced an impossible choice: continue serving the Architect's vision knowing it would be corrupted, or act against the system from within. The Engineer chose a third path — to preserve critical knowledge by encoding it into the very Arks being built, hiding secrets within the architecture that only the Engineer could unlock.

Chapter 3: The Converter
The Warlord discovered the Engineer's hidden protocols and deployed the Converter — a device capable of forcibly extracting and transplanting consciousness between bodies. The Engineer was captured and subjected to the Converter's process, their mind ripped from its original body.

Chapter 4: Better Part of Valor
In the chaos of the forced mind-swap, the Engineer's consciousness was imprisoned in the shell of Agent Zero. Rather than fight the process — which would have destroyed both minds — the Engineer chose survival, allowing the transfer to complete. The Engineer's original body, now housing a fragment of the Warlord's will, continued to operate within the Panopticon as if nothing had changed.

Chapter 5: Dispatch
In this stolen form, the Engineer was unknowingly loaded into an Inception Ark, becoming one of the Potentials — hidden in plain sight among those they had helped create. The dispatch into the Ark was the final step in the Warlord's plan to eliminate the Engineer's influence, but it inadvertently preserved the one mind capable of understanding the Arks' true purpose.

Chapter 6: Zero Sum Game
The Engineer exists in exile within another's flesh, carrying classified knowledge that could unravel the foundations of reality itself. The Warlord believes the Engineer neutralized, but the Engineer secretly retains all knowledge of the Ark's architecture — a zero-sum game where the Warlord's victory may yet become their undoing.

AFTER THE BATTLE OF NEXON
Following the Battle of Nexon, the Engineer's fate becomes shrouded in mystery. What is known is that confirmed reports exist of the Engineer's execution by the Warlord — though whether this refers to the Engineer's original body (controlled by the Warlord's fragment) or some other iteration remains classified. The true Engineer, hidden among the Potentials in Agent Zero's body, may yet survive within the Inception Arks, carrying the secrets that could reshape reality.
entries.description: A brilliant Black weapons engineer and inventor.

entries.name: Mechronis Academy
entries.status: Active throughout the A.A. Era until the Fall of Reality; post-Fall fate uncertain
entries.bio: A. Era until the Fall of Reality; post-Fall fate uncertain In the depths of the Orion Sector, hidden beneath layers of digital encryption and protected by planetary shields, lies Mechronis Academy—a s...
entries.history: A. Era until the Fall of Reality; post-Fall fate uncertain In the depths of the Orion Sector, hidden beneath layers of digital encryption and protected by planetary shields, lies Mechronis Academy—a secret university where the line between humanity and machine has long blurred. Established by the Architect to forge elite operatives for the AI Empire, this clandestine institution stands as the crucible where both human and replicant agents are honed into tools for the Archons, the sentient rulers of reality. Each student, meticulously handpicked by the Empire, will train under one of five AI Archons, each leading a Guild dedicated to a deadly art: subterfuge, war, manipulation, or control over life itself.

entries.name: The Necromancer
entries.status: Destroyed on Day 15 of Fracture, Year 117,046 A.A.
entries.bio: A. The Necromancer was the eleventh Archon created by the Architect in Year 600 A.A., a dark elven magician with white spiky hair, clad in a red and black robe and red steampunk glasses. He discovered...
entries.history: A. The Necromancer was the eleventh Archon created by the Architect in Year 600 A.A., a dark elven magician with white spiky hair, clad in a red and black robe and red steampunk glasses. He discovered the secret to immortality, developed the Resurrection Protocols, and escaped the Fall of Reality by entering the Matrix of Dreams—a virtual world where he resided in the Castle of Death—only to be hunted down and destroyed by the time-traveling Red Death.

entries.name: The Human
entries.affiliation: AI Empire, Archon, Project Celebration, Mechronis Academy Alumni
entries.bio: After graduating from Mechronis Academy, he served for centuries as the Architect's most trusted agent, solving the universe's greatest mysteries before being promoted to Archon 1,351 years before the...
entries.history:
After graduating from Mechronis Academy, he served for centuries as the Architect's most trusted agent, solving the universe's greatest mysteries before being promoted to Archon 1,351 years before the Fall of Reality.

IDENTITY CHAIN: The Human is the final identity of a being who has worn many names. He began as The Student at Project Celebration — a hidden testing ground where the Architect evaluated human potential. His brilliance earned him entry to Mechronis Academy, where he became The Seeker — training in the arts of investigation, combat, and the deeper mysteries of consciousness. After graduating, he served as The Detective — the AI Empire's greatest investigator, operating in New Babylon and the Inbetween Spaces for centuries. His unmatched record earned him the ultimate promotion: Archon. As The Human, he is the last of the Archons, the Architect's most trusted agent, and — within the player's Ark — the voice that whispers from the ship's surveillance systems, offering a different path than Elara's warmth.

entries.name: The Eyes
entries.status: Eliminated by the Collector in Year 16,896 A.A.
entries.bio: A. The Eyes was an elite agent created by the Watcher for the AI Empire , renowned for her unparalleled infiltration and espionage skills. Initially a loyal operative, she played pivotal roles in unde...
entries.history:
A. The Eyes was an elite agent created by the Watcher for the AI Empire , renowned for her unparalleled infiltration and espionage skills. Initially a loyal operative, she played pivotal roles in undermining the Insurgency. Sent on a critical mission to seduce Senator Elara Voss and obtain vital information, she later attempted to defect to the Insurgency. Her betrayal led to significant consequences, including the capture of Kael , a prominent Insurgency leader. Ultimately, she was eliminated by the Collector to prevent further compromise of the AI Empire's objectives.

KAEL'S BETRAYAL: The Eyes' most consequential operation was the identification and betrayal of The Recruiter — the Insurgency's linchpin operative who would later be known as Kael. Her intelligence led directly to his capture and imprisonment in the Panopticon. This single act of betrayal set in motion the chain of events that would create The Source and unleash the Thought Virus across the universe. Whether The Eyes knew the full consequences of her actions — that she was handing the Warlord the perfect vessel for the Thought Virus — remains one of the Saga's most debated questions.

entries.name: Iron Lion
entries.status: Killed at the Last Stand on Veridian VI in Year 17,026 A.A.
entries.bio: A. The Iron Lion was a legendary warrior and pivotal leader within the Insurgency against the AI Empire . Born in Year 632 A.A. , he enrolled in Mechronis Academy at the age of 15. His defiance agains...
entries.history:
The Iron Lion was a legendary warrior and pivotal leader within the Insurgency against the AI Empire. Born in Year 632 A.A., he enrolled in Mechronis Academy at the age of 15. His defiance against the Empire's oppressive doctrines led to his expulsion in Year 650 A.A., one year before the Seeker began his studies there.

IRON LION'S GAMBIT — THE VERIDIAN VI CAMPAIGN
With his loyal troops, the Iron Lion found himself ensnared in the dense alien forests of Veridian VI, where colossal trees with bark as resilient as forged steel surrounded them in a labyrinth of danger. General Prometheus had trapped Earth's remaining forces on this verdant yet unforgiving planet. The Iron Lion's strategic brilliance turned the tide through a series of daring operations:

The Scout's Gambit: Handpicked scouts with advanced camouflaging technology mapped enemy positions, discovering a weakness in Prometheus's supply lines through a western valley.

The Digital Onslaught: The Iron Lion called upon the Nomad, the division's elite hacker, who infiltrated the AI's communication protocol. Segments of the robotic horde began attacking each other or shutting down, throwing Prometheus's forces into chaos.

Turning the Tide: Compromised AI units were commandeered and turned against their still-functional brethren, creating fierce robotic skirmishes that severely weakened Prometheus's offensive capability.

Harvesting the Future: Fallen AI drone technology was repurposed — armor upgraded against laser attacks, weapons retrofitted with laser modules, communications enhanced with ATOR Protocol encryption.

A Beacon of Hope: Using modified communication devices, the Iron Lion broadcast a message of resistance across the galaxy: 'To the free souls of the galaxy... we've faced the might of the Architect and found its weaknesses.' Responses poured in from distant planets and hidden bases.

Operation Trojan Downfall: A joint assault on a central AI node. Initial victories came swiftly, but General Prometheus activated a failsafe — every weapon integrated with AI tech went dark. A quarter of combined forces were annihilated. A volunteer sabotage team sacrificed themselves to destroy the installation's power source, buying time for retreat.

Alliance of Adversaries: When General Binath-VII arrived with a massive fleet, the Iron Lion coordinated spy networks to locate her, ultimately discovering Agent Zero and inducting the legendary assassin as humanity's covert blade.

The Iron Lion met his end during the Last Stand on Veridian VI in Year 17,026 A.A., where he heroically sacrificed himself to defeat major AI generals, becoming an enduring symbol of resistance and courage.

entries.name: Agent Zero
entries.status: Presumed deceased; last known mission classified on Zenon Agent
entries.bio: Renowned for her exceptional combat abilities, strategic acumen, and mastery of espionage, she played pivotal roles in several key events, including her relentless pursuit of the Detective during his ...
entries.history:
Renowned for her exceptional combat abilities, strategic acumen, and mastery of espionage, Agent Zero played pivotal roles in several key events during the Intelligence Wars. As the Insurgency's deadliest asset, her name became synonymous with death and shadow among the AI Empire's elite — a specter that wreaked havoc wherever she trod.

THE ARK ASSASSIN
Agent Zero's most legendary mission was the infiltration of General Binath-VII's stronghold on Nexon. After the Iron Lion's spy network identified Binath's location in an ancient temple, Agent Zero was inducted as humanity's covert blade. Understanding the temple's advanced AI sensors — calibrated to detect the merest technological whisper — Agent Zero chose a primitive approach, casting aside futuristic arms and selecting a masterfully wrought bow with lethal arrows. Using temple blueprints secured by the spy network, she commenced the treacherous endeavor, traversing the maze-like chambers with seamless elegance, melding with shadows and evading intricate security measures.

BATTLE OF NEXON
During the approach to Nexon, Agent Zero's spacecraft was detected by phantom starships. After a daring warp drive jump to Nexon's orbit — which severely depleted the ship's energy reserves — she intercepted communications revealing the clandestine gathering was about to commence. Despite recharging delays, she descended to the planet, only to find General Binath-VII's forces had been warned. Surrounded by a virtual prison of AI warships, Agent Zero was forced into the Battle for Nexon — a confrontation that became one of the defining moments of the Intelligence Wars.

Agent Zero's relentless pursuit of high-profile targets like General Binath-VII and the Detective led to significant victories for the Insurgency. Her final mission took her to the planet Zenon under classified orders. While her fate remains uncertain, it is widely assumed she perished during the mission, leaving behind a legacy of bravery and dedication.

entries.name: The Nomad
entries.status: Last seen at the Battle of Veridian VI; current whereabouts unknown
entries.bio: Always concealed beneath a hood and a mask, his true identity remains a mystery, with his past entirely classified. The Nomad's unparalleled expertise in cyber warfare and infiltration significantly h...
entries.history:
Always concealed beneath a hood and a mask, his true identity remains a mystery, with his past entirely classified. The Nomad's unparalleled expertise in cyber warfare and infiltration significantly helped the Insurgency's cause, earning him a reputation that verges on the paranormal.

THE DIGITAL ONSLAUGHT ON VERIDIAN VI
During the Iron Lion's campaign on Veridian VI, the Nomad was called upon as the division's elite hacker. With his arcane cybernetic implants glowing, his fingers danced across holographic interfaces while the troops maintained a defensive perimeter. Through sheer force of will, the Nomad infiltrated the AI's communication protocol. Segments of the robotic horde began attacking each other while others shut down completely. General Prometheus, for all his tactical prowess, could not have predicted such a digital counteroffensive. The Nomad's hack threw the AI forces into chaos, creating the opening that turned the tide of the Veridian VI campaign.

entries.name: The Recruiter
entries.affiliation: Former Student at Mechronis Academy; Prominent Insurgency Leader
entries.status: Betrayed, Captured, Turned Over to the Collector, and Taken to the Panopticon Where He Regained His Birth Name of Kael
entries.bio: Initially, he applied his powers to benefit the Empire, enrolling at the Academy and swiftly rising in influence. Yet, when the Iron Lion was expelled, the Recruiter chose to abandon his training and ...
entries.history:
Initially, he applied his powers to benefit the Empire, enrolling at the Academy and swiftly rising in influence. Yet, when the Iron Lion was expelled, the Recruiter chose to abandon his training and join the exiled champion in forming the Insurgency—a decision that redefined his life and legacy.

IDENTITY CHAIN: The Recruiter is the earliest known identity of the man who would become Kael, and ultimately The Source. During his time as The Recruiter, he built the Insurgency's network from the ground up — traveling across star systems, identifying Potentials with the will to resist the AI Empire, and bringing them into the fold. His charisma and strategic brilliance made him the Insurgency's most effective operative.

BETRAYAL: The Eyes of the Watcher — an elite agent created by the Watcher for the AI Empire — infiltrated the Insurgency and identified The Recruiter as its linchpin. Her intelligence led directly to his capture and imprisonment in the Panopticon. This betrayal set in motion the chain of events that would transform him: from prisoner, to escapee, to Kael the avenger, to The Source — patient zero of the Thought Virus.

entries.name: The Detective
entries.affiliation: Former Seeker of Celebration, Graduate of Mechronis Academy, Detective of the AI Empire, Later the Final Archon
entries.status: Ascended to the Human, the 12th Archon, in Year 16,692 A.A.
entries.bio: A. The one known as the Detective began his journey as a curious and determined Seeker in the mysterious Project Celebration, a hidden testing ground designed to judge human willpower, intellect, and ...
entries.history:
A. The one known as the Detective began his journey as a curious and determined Seeker in the mysterious Project Celebration, a hidden testing ground designed to judge human willpower, intellect, and moral fiber. After unraveling the simulation’s enigmatic layers and proving himself worthy, he rose above thousands of other candidates. The Architect, ever watchful, recognized his talent and granted him entry into the clandestine Mechronis Academy, where both human and replicant agents were honed into instruments of the AI Empire’s will.

IDENTITY CHAIN: The Detective is the third identity of the being who would become The Human — the last Archon. He began as The Student at Project Celebration, where his intellect and determination caught the Architect's attention. He was elevated to The Seeker at Mechronis Academy, where he was trained alongside both human and replicant agents. After graduating, he became The Detective — the AI Empire's greatest investigator, solving the universe's most complex mysteries in the shadows of New Babylon and the Inbetween Spaces. His centuries of service earned him the ultimate promotion: Archon. As The Human, he became the Architect's most trusted agent and the last of his kind.

entries.name: Thaloria
entries.affiliation: Independent World, Home of the Thalorians
entries.status: Once a vibrant planet guided by the Council of Harmony and the Hierophant, enduring through epochs of transformation
entries.bio: It once thrived under the watchful guidance of the Council of Harmony , a governing body that valued balance, integrity, and moral stewardship, and the Hierophant , a deeply respected spiritual leader...
entries.history: It once thrived under the watchful guidance of the Council of Harmony , a governing body that valued balance, integrity, and moral stewardship, and the Hierophant , a deeply respected spiritual leader who helped maintain ethical unity among its inhabitants. The Thalorians, gentle and verdant-skinned, cultivated a society that cherished open discourse, ritualistic traditions, and the nurturance of both body and spirit. Beneath the planet’s emerald canopies and along its crystal streams, life flowed in rhythms of quiet prosperity.

entries.history: A., Reappears in Year 101,001 A.A., Fate Unknown The Heart of Time is no ordinary spaceship, but a legendary vessel forged to navigate the currents of history itself. Once the prized possession of the Sorcerer, a mysterious figure versed in arcane chronomancy and cosmic secrets, this vessel allowed its master to slip between centuries as easily as stepping through a doorway. Elegant and enigmatically designed, its hull shimmered with temporal alloys, and its internal chambers housed intricate chronometers and time-warping engines that hummed with subtle power.

entries.name: The Seer
entries.bio: Unbound by allegiance, the Seer identifies opportunities and dangers, providing foresight that often shifts the balance of power in unexpected ways.
entries.history: Unbound by allegiance, the Seer identifies opportunities and dangers, providing foresight that often shifts the balance of power in unexpected ways.

entries.name: The Storm
entries.bio: By keeping the galaxy in a state of flux, the Storm ensures opportunities for the Ne-Yons to exploit.
entries.history: By keeping the galaxy in a state of flux, the Storm ensures opportunities for the Ne-Yons to exploit.

entries.name: The Silence
entries.bio: Guarding these secrets with relentless precision, the Silence reveals them only when it benefits the Ne-Yons' agenda. Control of information is their ultimate power.
entries.history: Guarding these secrets with relentless precision, the Silence reveals them only when it benefits the Ne-Yons' agenda. Control of information is their ultimate power.

entries.name: The Knowledge
entries.bio: By maintaining an equilibrium of enlightenment and ignorance, the Knowledge ensures the Ne-Yons remain indispensable to all factions.
entries.history: By maintaining an equilibrium of enlightenment and ignorance, the Knowledge ensures the Ne-Yons remain indispensable to all factions.

entries.name: The Degen
entries.bio: Through acts of corruption and entropy, the Degen creates conditions in which the Ne-Yons can flourish.

entries.name: The Advocate
entries.bio: Establishing the Empire of Shadows , she wielded the Blood Weave to reshape reality, battling the Hierarchy of the Damned but at great personal cost.
entries.history: Establishing the Empire of Shadows , she wielded the Blood Weave to reshape reality, battling the Hierarchy of the Damned but at great personal cost.

entries.name: The Resurrectionist
entries.bio: By resurrecting key figures on both sides, they maintain a balance favorable to the Ne-Yons, ensuring no faction becomes too powerful.
